mission.title: fluentd: JSON parser plugin can sometimes output records as strings rather than `Hash`
mission.description: JSON parser plugin can sometimes output records as strings rather than `Hash`. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify fluentd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluentd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for error: `"undefined method `map' for #<String:0x00007fdbb6b32308>\nDid you mean?  tap" location="/usr/local/bundle/gems/fluent-pl`

steps.title: Review fluentd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluentd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl get configmap -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluentd
```
### Describe the bug

The JSON parser plugin can sometimes emit records as a string rather than as a `Hash`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for JSON parser plugin can sometimes output records as strings…
steps.description:
Sorry for the delay!

This is not only an issue for `filter_parser`, but for all plugins that can use `parser_json`.

So we should fix `parser_json`.

https://github.com/fluent/fluentd/blob/d5df992d844af38233c4d3788de714d644ddb6c4/lib/fluent/plugin/parser_json.rb#L72-L81

This should return `nil` when the parsed result is not a `Hash`.

The root cause of this is that sometimes the Ruby library does not parse a given string as `Hash`.

```rb
require "json"
JSON.load('"hoge"')
=> "hoge"
```

I

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm JSON parser plugin can sometimes output records… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluentd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that `"undefined method `map' for #<String:0x00007fdbb6b32308>\nDid you mean?  tap" location="/usr/local/bundle/gems/fluent-pl` no longer appears in logs.

resolution.summary:
Sorry for the delay!

This is not only an issue for `filter_parser`, but for all plugins that can use `parser_json`.

So we should fix `parser_json`.

https://github.com/fluent/fluentd/blob/d5df992d844af38233c4d3788de714d644ddb6c4/lib/fluent/plugin/parser_json.rb#L72-L81

This should return `nil` when the parsed result is not a `Hash`.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluentd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
